description_long: This object is a large solar energy infrastructure installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels arranged in a grid formation. The panels are mounted on a robust, likely metallic support structure that elevates them above a flat base, which may represent a rooftop or ground platform. The panels are tilted at a consistent angle, optimizing exposure to sunlight, and are divided into two main banks separated by a central walkway or service area. The structure includes several vertical elements that could be electrical boxes, service posts, or mounting supports. The color palette is dominated by shades of gray, black, and some red accents, suggesting metallic and synthetic materials. The overall design is industrial and functional, suitable for urban or commercial environments.
description: A large, grid-arranged solar panel installation on a sturdy platform with tilted panels and metallic supports, featuring service areas and vertical utility elements.
description_view_0: This view shows the full length of the installation, highlighting the two main banks of tilted solar panels and the central service area.
description_view_1: From this side, the tilt of the solar panels is prominent, and the vertical support structures are more visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the underside of the panels, the supporting frame, and the red accent along the edge of the base.
description_view_3: This side view mirrors view 1, emphasizing the panel tilt and the separation between the two main banks.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, azimuth direction not clearly specified, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with metallic (likely aluminum or steel) support frame
attribute_array_configuration: two parallel rows of panels, each row containing approximately 6-8 panels, separated by a central walkway
